Just seconds before officers opened fire, Bennett can be seen on CCTV pointing a gun to his head on the light rail tracks, then pointing the gun toward officers.
After Bennett was shot, police moved in immediately and rendered medical aid. The handgun he was holding wasn't loaded.

As of publication, Bennett remains hospitalized and has been charged with three counts of second-degree murder for the Minneapolis shootings. He's facing ineligible possession of a firearm and second-degree assault for the St. Paul incident.
Bennett has eight prior felony convictions, including five for aggravated robbery.
